title: Microsoft restructures Xbox with mass layoffs and sale of five studios
summary: The company seeks to improve its margins in the face of competition and redefines its first-party games strategy.

title: Nvidia's Kyber NVL144 AI rack system delayed until 2028
summary: Manufacturing issues in the motherboards delay the next generation of AI infrastructure, although Nvidia denies it.

title: AMD launches the Ryzen AI Halo PC at $3,999 as an AI development station
summary: The system offers performance similar to Nvidia's DGX Spark in a compact and affordable format.

title: MSI validates Chinese CXMT memory at record DDR5-8000+ speeds on AMD boards
summary: New BIOS updates allow achieving frequencies of 8200 MHz in memory manufactured by CXMT, competing with major manufacturers.

title: Valve releases Windows drivers for Steam Machine and shows an e-ink front panel
summary: In an interview, engineers explain the price, component shortage, and Windows support.

title: Sony headphones work as a free head tracker in simulators thanks to an open-source app
summary: The app translates sensor data into camera movements for hundreds of racing and flight games.

title: Nintendo Switch 2 introduces battery revisions while the original Switch is retired from Europe in 2027
summary: The new console complies with EU regulations while the current generation ends sales due to regulations.

title: They create Tom Riddle's diary by combining a reMarkable tablet with artificial intelligence
summary: An artistic project uses an AI model to generate magical text on the e-ink screen.

title: Critical 16-year-old vulnerability in KVM allows virtual machine escape on x86 systems
summary: The Januscape flaw affects all Linux kernels and allows guests to execute code on the host.

title: China forces its AI platforms to remove chatbots with human personalities
summary: Authorities ban affective virtual assistants to prevent users' emotional dependence.

title: The first autonomous ransomware JADEPUFFER demonstrates the evolution of automated threats
summary: Although the attack was completely AI-driven, it still required a manual entry point.

title: Samsung posts record profits in its semiconductor division and its foundry returns to profitability
summary: The AI boom boosts the South Korean firm's profits, surpassing Nvidia in profitability.

title: The competitive advantage of AI models collapses: they barely remain at the top for weeks
summary: Rapid evolution and competition drastically reduce margins and the duration of technical leadership.

title: Microsoft can track users through a unique device identifier in Windows
summary: The GDID allows the company to monitor activity even after resetting the operating system.

title: Amazon shuts down Mechanical Turk, the original human intelligence service
summary: The platform that outsourced simple tasks to human workers ends its operations after years of decline.

title: German electric drone breaks world speed record at 434 mph (698 km/h)
summary: Designed as an anti-aircraft interceptor, the drone far exceeds the official record and seeks Guinness certification.

title: Huawei enters South Korean AI chip market with Atlas Ascend 950 superclusters
summary: The new systems offer three times the inference performance of the H20 GPU at a quarter of the cost.

title: Wisconsin neighbors sue Microsoft over noise and light pollution from its AI data center
summary: The class-action lawsuit alleges that the $7.3 billion facility generates noise, vibrations, and excessive light.

title: Reddit uses language models to combat AI-generated misinformation
summary: The platform employs AI tools to detect deceptive content created by the same technology.

title: Netflix moves away from the binge-watching model and bets on weekly releases
summary: The platform reconsiders its content strategy in the face of changing consumption habits.
